description: Fig. B.1. Quantity h − 1 in complete markets. The figure shows deviations of h from 1, where h is defined in Proposition 5, in the complete markets case of habit-formation preferences with stochastic risk aversion and constant information quality (Model 4). For these plots, we vary one state variable at a time, keeping the rest at their steady state values. For these computations we use s¯=ln⁡(1−0.93).

Risk-free rate and price of risk – Static analysis (η = 0.247). The graphs show the risk-free rate, as given by Lemma 4, and the price of risk, as given by Lemma 5, of the model with constant information quality (ρ) in terms of ζ = η/ρ and for several values of the utility curvature parameter (γ). The rest of the parameters are as in Table 2. The parameter ζ ranges from η to 1. Panels A and B show the risk-free rate for the state μ˜=μc and for β = 0.99. Panels C and D show the price of risk for the state μ˜=μ. For Panels A and C the parameter θ depends on γ and given by θ = 25 ÷ γ − 1. For Panels B and D, θ is equal to 15.

The pd-ratio and the risk-free rate (rf)– Model 1. The graphs show the pd-ratio and the risk-free rate (rf) as functions of the state variables in Model 1. For each graph, we vary the corresponding state variable, while the rest of the variables are kept at their steady states. The gray areas show the unconditional distributions of the state variables. Where we plot two quantities, the “left” corresponds to the left axis and “right” to the right axis.
